characters.description: The AnimatorAppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(2020, 1 episode)Segment(s)Yakko, Wakko, and DotFirst Episode"Yakko Amakko"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Nancy Cartwright The Animatoris an artist who appears in theAnimaniacsrebootcartoon "Yakko Amakko." She is voiced byNancy Cartwright. The Animator has a felt green beanie hat, with brown hair, pink glasses, an "ironic" 90s T-shirt under her dark blue coat with white sleeves, her Ship Cove pants and green shoes.

characters.name: B.R.A.I.N.
characters.description: B.R.A.I.N.B.R.A.I.N.'s normal formGeneral InformationFull NameBinary Rodent with Artificially Intelligent NanotechnologyRelationships•The Brain("father")•Romy("adoptive brother")•Desireeandher unnamed husband("adoptive grandparents")AppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(2020) (1 episode)Segment(s)Pinky and the BrainFirst Episode"Ex-Mousina"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Maurice LaMarche TheBinary Rodent with Artificially Intelligent Nanotechnology,orB.R.A.I.N.for short, is a character from theAnimaniacsreboot. He's Brain's robotic son and the main antagonist of "Ex-Mousina." Realizing how capable artificial intelligence is, Brain decides to build a robot of himself to aid him in taking over the world. Upon introducing B.R.A.I.N. to Pinky, the Brain decides to spend some time bonding with his new creation.

characters.name: Babs Bunny
characters.description: Barbara Anne "Babs" Bunnyis a main character onTiny Toon Adventures. She has white and pink fur, brown eyes (blue contacts) and wears a yellow medium-sleeved blouse, purple knee-length skirt, frilly white panties (on Fridays only) and purple bows near the tops of her ears. Her best friend isBuster Bunny, who she may have a crush on. Her other friends includeShirley the Loon,Fifi La Fume,Plucky Duck, andHamton J. Pig. Her rivals areDizzy Deviland Montana Max, who she usually overcomes by disguising herself. She is voiced byTress MacNeille, who later voicedDot WarneronAnimaniacs. Babs makes occassional cameos onAnimaniacs.

characters.name: Baloney the Dinosaur
characters.description: Baloney the DinosaurGeneral InformationFull NameBaloney the DinosaurAppearancesTV Series•Animaniacs(1993)•Pinky and the Brain(1995)•Pinky, Elmyra and the Brain(1998)MoviesWakko's Wish(1999)GamesAnimaniacs Game Pack(1997)Segment(s)Yakko, Wakko, and DotFirst Episode"Baloney & Kids"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Jeff BennettQuotesCatchphrases"Yum, yum, doodle-dum!" Baloney the Dinosauris a large Tarbosaurus who is a parody of the titular character in theBarneyfranchise. He was first introduced in the first-season episode "Baloney and Kids". He is voiced byJeff Bennett. Baloney is happy, huggy and loves to sing. He is oblivious to certain situations, still managing to be positive and happy, such as when the Warner siblings dropped anvils on his head and pretended that he ran out of oxygen in space. Baloney is one of the few people capable of truly terrifying Yakko, Wakko and Dot, largely due to being impervious to their violently-physical brand of humor and his inability to understand how much they despise him. Pinky, Elmyra and the Brainrevealed his dark side: he has a strong dislike for cheating or swearing. He andElmyraboth smashedThe Brainwhile calling him a "naughty potty-mouth" due to being unable to grasp his (swearless) verbosity.

characters.name: Beanie the Brain-Dead Bison
characters.description: Beanie the Brain-Dead BisonAppearancesTV Series•Animaniacs(1993)•Pinky and the Brain(1995)Segment(s)Slappy SquirrelPortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Avery Schreiber Beanie the Brain-Dead Bison(also calledBeanie the Cerebrally Challenged Bison) is one ofSlappy Squirrel's foes from her cartoons. As per his name, he is a few ants short of a picnic and a slow wit. He was voiced by the lateAvery Schreiber.

characters.name: Benedict
characters.description: BenedictGeneral InformationFull NameBenedictAppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(2020)Segment(s)•Pinky and the Brain•Yakko, Wakko, and DotFirst Episode"How to Brain Your Dragon"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Fred Tatasciore Benedictis a dragon that appears intheAnimaniacsreboot, debuting inthe second episode. He is an aspiring actor. He is voiced byFred Tatasciore. Benedict has a flair for the dramatic. He is very sophisticated and cares more about his performance than anything else, and tries his best to impress the audience. He is often shown practicing. In "Warner's Ark", Benedict states that he does not need a love interest as he "treads the bonds alone".

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Bert is a cannibal which was mentioned when Wakko was explaining his Dimensional storage.

characters.name: Bill Clinton
characters.description: Bill Clinton“While Bill Clinton plays the sax!”General InformationFull NameWilliam Jefferson “Bill” ClintonRelationshipsHillary (wife, 1975-)AppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(1993-1998)Pinky and the Brain(1995-1998)Animaniacs(2020)First EpisodeAnimaniacs Theme SongPortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Frank Welker Bill Clintonis the 42nd President of the United States (1993-2001), husband ofHillary Clinton, and the incumbent President during the 5-year run ofAnimaniacs,Pinky and the Brain, and other Warner Bros. shows, so he was often featured in them. During his appearances on the show he was voiced byFrank Welker. Clinton is an avid saxophone musician (as one of the Animaniacs theme song references) and made a famous appearance onThe Arsenio Hall Showduring his first presidential campaign.

characters.description: Bill NyeGeneral InformationFull NameWilliam Sanford NyeAppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(2020) (1 episode)Segment(s)Yakko, Wakko, and DotFirst Episode"Gold Meddlers"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Eric Bauza William "Bill" Sanford Nyeis an American mechanical engineer, science communicator, and television presenter.

characters.name: Billie
characters.description: BillieGeneral InformationFull NameBillieRelationships•The Brain(friend; one-sided ex-attraction on his side)•Pinky(friend/love interest; one-sided attraction on her side)•Snowball(ex-partner)AppearancesTV Series•Animaniacs(1993) (1 episode)•Pinky and the Brain(1995)PrintPinky and the Brain(DC Comic)(1996)Segment(s)Pinky and the BrainFirst EpisodeAnimaniacs:"The World Can Wait"Pinky and the Brain:"Brain Noir"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Tress MacNeilleQuotesCatchphrases"Natch!"  Billieis a gene-spliced lab mouse fromAcme Labswho appeared in the originalAnimaniacsandPinky and the Brain.Though an experiment gave her super intelligence, she is very oblivious and "ditzy," more similar toPinkythanthe Brainin those terms. Ironically, she is often the object of Brain's affection, though their feelings for each other are ultimately conflicted due to their own desires getting in the way. Billie is very airheaded and ditzy in a similar way toPinky. Though she has more focus than he does, they have a similar sense of humor, way of speaking, and enjoyment of "childish" activities. However, she does not hold the same love for being hurt and ordered around that he does.[1]She and Pinky seem to have the similar trait of falling in love quickly, as in "Brain Noir" she was stated to have dated both Brain and Snowball before crushing on Pinky. (Though, in her previous appearance, she had shown no interest in Brain.[2]) At the same time, she can be oblivious to the intentions and feelings of others, such as not understanding Brain's affection for her while also not realizing that Pinky doesn't hold the same romantic feelings that she does. In the same episode, she is shown to be ruthless and cunning when she needs be. At the end of "The World Can Wait," anAcme Labsexperiment gives her a hyperintelligence, and though she remains very bubbleheaded in personality, she is stated to be even smarter than Brain in terms of intellect, swiftly solving a mathematical problem he had been pondering for months. Brain believes that she might be able to take over the world before them if she wanted to. Billie seems to pay a bit of attention to her appearance, sometimes seen filing her nails[2]and portrayed as vain in an alternate reality.[3]

characters.name: Bobby
characters.description: BobbyGeneral InformationFull NameBobbyRelationshipsLana(girlfriend)Pesto,Squit(Goodfeathers)AppearancesTV Series•Animaniacs(1993)•Pinky and the Brain(1995)•Animaniacs(2020) (2 episodes)MoviesWakko's Wish(1999)Segment(s)GoodfeathersFirst EpisodeThe Monkey Song/Goodfeathers: The BeginningPortrayalsVoice Actor(s)John MarianoJeff Glen Bennett(singing voice)QuotesCatchphrases"You walking with me?""The Godpigeon!""Batta-bing!" Bobbyis the de-facto leader of the Goodfeather pigeons, whose character was inspired by the film roles of Robert De Niro (such as Travis Bickle, Jake LaMotta, and Jimmy Conway). He tends to get along withSquitandPesto, but always laughs his head off whenever the two end up getting into a scuffle. He is also the only one who can translate the Godpigeon's elderly mumbling. Bobby has a girlfriend in the Girlfeather Lana, although at one point their relationship seemed to be at risk by the Godpigeon himself, as the Godpigeon courted her. Bobby is the brains of the operation in the way that he comes up with ideas that the other goodfeathers follow and succeed with. In "Dough Dough Boys", he came up with the idea of colliding the blimp with the plane to avoid capture, and inTa Da Dump, Ta Da Dump, Ta Da Dump Dump Dump," Bobby came up with the idea of disguising as cans to get unstuck from plastic. These examples clearly show Bobby's aptitude in leadership and smarts compared to the other Goodfeathers. He is also capable of avoiding both Pesto's raging temper and Squit's blind optimism. He is noticeably one of the few people capable of keeping Pesto's temper in check, just by glaring at him. Despite this, he is at times content to let either Pesto or Squit fall to their own devices, as their fighting amuses him. He is approximately the average of Squit and Pesto in terms of aggressiveness, confidence and calmness. In "Hiccup," Bobby would get slightly angrier with each thing going wrong, while in many episodes, Pesto would get much angrier with little provocation, and Squit would not get angry at all. In that way, Bobby is the well-rounded, calm-headed, smooth-talker leader of the goodfeathers.

Bobby has a girlfriend in the Girlfeather Lana, although at one point their relationship seemed to be at risk by the Godpigeon himself, as the Godpigeon courted her. Bobby is the brains of the operation in the way that he comes up with ideas that the other goodfeathers follow and succeed with. In "Dough Dough Boys", he came up with the idea of colliding the blimp with the plane to avoid capture, and inTa Da Dump, Ta Da Dump, Ta Da Dump Dump Dump," Bobby came up with the idea of disguising as cans to get unstuck from plastic. These examples clearly show Bobby's aptitude in leadership and smarts compared to the other Goodfeathers. He is also capable of avoiding both Pesto's raging temper and Squit's blind optimism. He is noticeably one of the few people capable of keeping Pesto's temper in check, just by glaring at him. Despite this, he is at times content to let either Pesto or Squit fall to their own devices, as their fighting amuses him. He is approximately the average of Squit and Pesto in terms of aggressiveness, confidence and calmness. In "Hiccup," Bobby would get slightly angrier with each thing going wrong, while in many episodes, Pesto would get much angrier with little provocation, and Squit would not get angry at all. In that way, Bobby is the well-rounded, calm-headed, smooth-talker leader of the goodfeathers.

characters.name: Boo-Boo
characters.description: Boo-BooAppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(2020) (1 episode)First Episode"Suffragette City" Boo-Boo Bearis a Hanna-Barbera cartoon character onThe Yogi Bear Show. Boo-Boo is an anthropomorphic bear cub who wears a blue. Boo-Boo isYogi Bear's constant companion, and often acts as his conscience. He tries (usually unsuccessfully) to keep Yogi from doing things he should not do. Him and Yogi made their firstAnimaniacsappearance in "Suffragette City."

characters.name: Boystorm Boys
characters.description: The Boystorm BoysareCora's favorite band who only appeared in "My Super Sour Sixteen". They arrived late due to their flight being cancelled so they have to drive to the Warner Bros. studio to celebrate Cora's sweet 16. Unfortunately, the sound stages were destroyed before they got there, soDotasks them for an after-party at the Water Tower, where they fix the leaky pipes and sing "Righty Tighty Lefty Loosie" while the Warners,Nora, and Cora dance with them. One of the boys is named Leon and they are all voiced byJake Green.

characters.name: The Brain
characters.actor_name: Maurice LaMarcheSee the "Voices" section for more information
characters.description: The Brain, or simplyBrain, is an intelligent megalomaniac mouse and is one of the two major protagonists ofthePinky and The Brainsegmentsas well as it'sspin-offshows. He is a mouse that harbors a long-lasting desire to take over theworld, for which he makes nightlyattemptsat doing. His assistant,Pinky, is more dim-witted and excitable, which often irritates Brain, though he is secretly fond of him. He is voiced byMaurice LaMarche. Brain is a short, anthropomorphic white-furred mouse; in theAnimaniacsreboot, his fur is more off-white. He has large ears, a red nose, and pink skin on his paws and crooked tail, as well as matching pink eyes. He doesn't often stand up straight, instead hunching his shoulders slightly. Notably, he has a very large and wide head, housing his abnormally large brain. Described in the intro as a "genius", Brain is incredibly intelligent and believes himself to be quite logical (inventing such items asthe Human suitand theNoodle Noggin dolls). He tends to make himself appear unemotional and cold, and often speaks in a snarky, deadpan tone. He often makes incredibly complicated plans in hopes of taking over the world and establishing himself as its ruler. However, this plans will often fail due to an overlooked flaw, either something he neglected to notice or an incident caused byPinky. However many times this occurs, he refuses to be deterred from his goal, and gets to work preparing for the following night's plan. Brain does not want to take over the world simply to be a dictator; he believes that he would be able to be an effective ruler due to his increased intelligence. He actually is shown to be a good leader when not focused on world domination, as he stands up to cruelty and easily inspires others.[3]However, Brain's traumatic childhood led to him gaining a control complex; he becomes nervous and upset when he does not have control over a situation and often acts rashly in an attempt to gain his position back. This can be seen in his frustration when plans go awry, and most obviously in "Welcome to the Jungle," in which his lack of ability to survive in the wild drove him into a breakdown. This also leads him to be incredibly authoritative over others, such as Pinky andJulia, often to both their detriment and his. Despite being vastly intelligent, Brain does not have great social skills; he is often blunt, awkward or confused when talking to others. He can also become easily annoyed by what he perceives as stupid or inane actions, and will get quickly angered if insulted. He can sometimes become jealous when Pinky becomes more popular than him in the process of their night's plan, though he will later regret his actions towards his friend. Brain is not good at showing his emotions, instead suppressing them in order to better make his plans. However, as mentioned, he is shown to panic when out of control, and he also often shows affection to those he is close to, such as Pinky(see below section)andSnowball, whom he tr

characters.name: Brain's Father
characters.description: Brain's FatherGeneral InformationRelationshipsDesiree(wife)The Brain(son)Romy(grandson)B.R.A.I.N.("adoptive grandson")Julia(ex-daughter-in-law)Unnamed co-grandfatherandco-grandmotherAppearancesTV SeriesPinky and the Brain(1995)First Episode"The Visit"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Ernest Borgnine "You got a squeak here that would wake upBroderick Crawford."- Brain's Father Brain's Fatheris a white field mouse who appears inPinky and the Brain.He is voiced byErnest Borgnine. Brain's father is very stubborn and a little patronizing, and often talks over his son and snaps at him to listen to him and his mother; he doesn't seem to have processed that Brain has grown since they saw him last, threatening him with time-outs and allowance cuts. While they express disappointment in Brain's "life choices," they busy themselves cleaning up his cage and fixing his wheel. He and his wife also very much likePinky, and find him amusing. After they are sent away at the end of the episode, Brain mentions that his parents invited him and Pinky over for Thanksgiving, hinting that they care about their son, at least a bit.

characters.name: Buddy
characters.description: BuddyGeneral InformationFull NameBuddyAppearancesTV SeriesAnimaniacs(1993)Segment(s)Yakko, Wakko, and DotFirst Episode"The Warners' 65th Anniversary Special"PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Jim CummingsQuotesCatchphrases[Creepy Laugh]  Buddyis an olderLooney Tunescharacter who appears in the episode "The Warners 65th Anniversary Special." While it seems that in both on and off screen, Buddy is shown to be a happy, cheery toon who always smiles, even when he's in pain. However during the 65th episode, Buddy (while in cloak form) is shown to be evil and seek revenge on the Warners for ruining his career. However, he reformed after he was moved by the Warners' speech about thanking him for giving them the start of their career.

characters.name: Bugs Bunny
characters.actor_name: Greg Burson(OriginalAnimaniacs)Mel Blanc(archival footage in "Critical Condition")
characters.description: Bugs BunnyGeneral InformationFull NameBugs BunnyAppearancesTV Series•Animaniacs(1993)•Pinky and the Brain(1995)•Animaniacs(2020)Segment(s)•Yakko, Wakko, and Dot•Slappy SquirrelFirst Episode"De-Zanitized" (Poster)"Critical Condition" (Physical)PortrayalsVoice Actor(s)Greg Burson(OriginalAnimaniacs)Mel Blanc(archival footage in "Critical Condition")QuotesCatchphrasesEh, what’s up doc? Bugs Bunnyis aLooney Tunesstar who occasionally appears in theAmblin/Warner Bros.cartoons. OnTiny Toon Adventures, he is a teacher at ACME Looniversity and mentor to Buster Bunny. OnAnimaniacs, he makes a few appearances. He is often seen on portraits around the Warner Bros. studio. In addition, a figurine of him is sometimes seen on a phoneYakko,Wakko, andDotown. InVideo Review, he appears on the VHS cover ofBugsy, asking the viewers if they expected Warren Beatty to appear instead. He later joins in on the closing musical number. InThe Warners 65th Anniversary Special, he is interviewed to recount his experiences with the Warners. Apparently, it was him who advised the Warners to get anagent.

characters.name: Bumbie
characters.description: Bumbieis a cartoon deer who appeared in the episode "Bumbie's Mom." He is the star of the film-within-the-show,Bumbie the Dearest Deer.

characters.description: Bumpo Bassett with his grandfather Bumpo Bassetis a basset hound who appears in "Smell Ya Later" He isStinkbomb'sgrandson.
